title: bumelia reclinata var. rufotomentosa
intro: Bumelia reclinata var. rufotomentosa, also known as the red-flowered reclinata, is a plant species in the family Sapotaceae, native to Mexico and Central America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which contains flavonoids and phenolic compounds that exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. The bark and leaves of Bumelia reclinata var. rufotomentosa are traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, offering potential health benefits such as reduced inflammation and improved immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant's bark and leaves are often dried and used in teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
